intro: Porterandia castaneofulva, also known as the Indian Porterandia, is a plant species that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Porterandia castaneofulva include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Indian medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat various health conditions, including coughs and colds, as well as to promote digestion and reduce fever.
